mission.quote: 10 years is the minimum for a bicycle – and for a laptop too!
mission.paragraph1: As a non-profit association, we have been changing the way technology is used since 2003. Our mission is clear: extend the lifespan of IT devices and reduce e-waste through repair, refurbishment and sustainable practices.
mission.paragraph2: In our premises – a former bank – we have created a meeting place where technology and sustainability come together. Our approach combines hardware recycling with open source software to create sustainable IT solutions for people and the environment.

hero.title: Giving technology a second life
hero.subtitle: For over 20 years we have been fighting against the premature decommissioning of computers and promoting sustainable IT practices.

items.question: What's the difference between a user account and a membership?
items.answer: A user account is all you need for the marketplace, IT help, and workshops — it's free. An association membership gives you voting rights and makes you officially part of the membership structure. Most users never become members — that's completely fine.

items.question: What do I actually get as a member?
items.answer: Voting rights on association decisions and a place on the official membership list. We don't promise exclusive perks — our services are open to everyone. You become a member because you want to support the association.

items.question: How does joining work?
items.answer: Fill in the form, transfer the annual fee — done. You're a member immediately. No waiting, no approval process.

items.question: How do I pay the annual membership fee?

items.question: Can I leave at any time?
items.answer: Yes. A simple informal email to the committee is all it takes. Annual fees already paid are not refunded.

meta.title: FAQ – Frequently Asked Questions
meta.description: Answers to common questions about Linux & Open Source, repair, sustainability and e-waste.

hero.title: Frequently Asked Questions
hero.subtitle: Our answers on Linux & Open Source, repair, sustainability and responsible use of resources – in the spirit of our mission against e-waste.

faq.notListed: Can't find your question? We're happy to help in person.

items.q: Do you only work with open-source software?
items.a: No. Open source is our preferred route – for its transparency, security and longevity. Where it makes sense, we also use or integrate proprietary software, always with a focus on usefulness and sustainability.

items.q: Why is open source important to you?
items.a: Open source strengthens self-determination and repairability. It reduces dependencies and makes it possible to use devices for longer – key levers against e-waste.

items.q: What does 'Right to Repair' mean to you?
items.a: We advocate for repairable hardware, documented processes and available spare parts. This extends the lifespan of devices and preserves resources.

items.q: Do you only work with Linux, or also Windows and macOS?
items.a: We work exclusively with Linux and open-source software. We don't install proprietary operating systems (Windows/macOS). Very rarely we make exceptions when a goal demonstrably can't be achieved with open-source tools.

items.q: Do you recommend Linux for everyone?
items.a: Linux is our standard because it's resource-friendly, secure and sustainable. If proprietary tools are absolutely required, we evaluate alternatives – and discuss rare exceptions openly.

items.q: Do you support migration to Linux or open-source alternatives?
items.a: Yes. From pilot projects to full roll-out, we guide individuals, nonprofits and businesses – including training and ongoing support.

items.q: Do you repair devices that weren't bought from you?
items.a: Yes. We repair laptops, PCs and peripherals from a wide range of manufacturers – regardless of where they were purchased.

items.q: Do you repair Apple devices?
items.a: Hardware, yes – provided spare parts are available and a repair makes sense. OS installations are Linux-only; we don't install macOS or Windows.

items.q: How long do repairs take, and what do they cost?
items.a: That depends on the fault and the parts needed. We'll provide a quote on request and keep you fully informed of your options.

items.q: Do you offer data recovery and secure data erasure?
items.a: Yes. We recover data to the best of our ability and securely erase storage media on request using recognised standards (e.g. open-source tools).

items.q: Can I just drop in without an appointment?
items.a: Absolutely. For complex cases or business appointments, a quick message in advance is helpful.

items.q: How do you concretely reduce e-waste?
items.a: Through repairs, component reuse, refurbishing and responsible recycling. We also advise on long-lasting setups and resource-efficient software.

items.q: Do you accept old devices?
items.a: Yes, provided reuse or responsible recycling is possible. We inspect devices on the spot and decide transparently whether they'll be reused or dismantled.

items.q: Do you use second-hand spare parts?
items.a: Where technically sensible, we use tested second-life parts. That saves resources and can cut costs – with no compromise on function.

items.q: Why refurbished instead of new?
items.a: Refurbished devices extend useful life, save CO₂ and raw materials. With the right upgrades, they're often more than good enough – at fair prices.

items.q: Do you stock refurbished devices?
items.a: Yes. We offer carefully checked and refurbished devices with warranty – transparently documented.

items.q: What is the REVAMPED certification?
items.a: REVAMPED marks sustainable, repairable builds with documented provenance, clear upgrade paths and quality assurance.

items.q: Is there a warranty on your devices?
items.a: Yes. Refurbished devices come with a warranty. We state the details transparently for each product.

items.q: How do you handle my data during repairs?
items.a: Confidentially and minimally. We only access data when needed for diagnosis, and protect storage media technically and organisationally.

items.q: Do you offer certified data erasure?
items.a: We offer secure erasure procedures with a record. Just let us know what level of security you need.

items.q: Do you offer workshops or training?
items.a: Yes. Topics include: Linux introduction, repair basics, sustainable IT, open-source tools – for beginners through to advanced users.

items.q: How can I get involved or support you?
items.a: As a volunteer, partner or donor. Sharing your knowledge is also very welcome – together we create real impact.

section1.title: 1. Scope
section1.body: These Terms and Conditions (T&Cs) apply to all services and offers of {legalName}, {address}.

section2.title: 2. Services
section2.intro: {orgName} is a non-profit association committed to the sustainable refurbishment and reuse of IT equipment. Our services include:
items: Refurbishment and sale of second-hand IT equipment

section3.title: 3. Prices and Payment
section3.body: All prices are in Swiss Francs (CHF) including VAT, unless stated otherwise. Payment terms are specified for each offer.

section4.title: 4. Conclusion of Contract
section4.body: A contract is concluded by placing an order via the platform, in the shop or by e-mail, followed by confirmation from {orgName}. Representations on the website constitute a non-binding invitation to submit an offer.

section5.title: 5. Right of Withdrawal
section5.body: For in-store purchases there is no statutory right of withdrawal, as Swiss law does not provide for this in physical shops. For online purchases, we grant a voluntary right of return of 14 days from receipt, provided the goods are returned unused and in their original packaging.

section6.title: 6. Warranty
section6.body: {orgName} provides a warranty on refurbished devices in accordance with the applicable legal provisions. Exact warranty terms are communicated at the point of purchase. For devices sold via the P2P marketplace by third parties, {orgName} assumes no warranty.

section7.title: 7. Liability
section7.body: {orgName} is liable within the scope of the applicable legal provisions. Liability for minor negligence is excluded to the extent permitted by law.

section8.title: 8. Data Protection
section8.body: Personal data is processed in accordance with our privacy policy. Registered users can export their data at any time.

section9.title: 9. Final Provisions
section9.body: Should individual provisions of these T&Cs prove invalid, the validity of the remaining provisions is not affected. Swiss law applies. The place of jurisdiction is Zurich, Switzerland.

section10.title: 10. Changes
section10.body: {orgName} reserves the right to change these T&Cs at any time. The current version is available on this page. Legal information about the provider can be found in the legal notice.

meta.title: Privacy Policy
meta.description: Privacy Policy of {orgName}

section2.title: 2. Collection and Processing of Personal Data
section2.intro: We collect personal data when you register on our platform, use our services, or contact us. This includes:
items: Contact details (for enquiries and appointment bookings)

section3.title: 3. Purpose of Data Processing
items: Providing and managing your user account
Carrying out repair orders and services
Communication about our offers (only with your consent)

section4.title: 4. Data Sharing
section4.body: Your data will not be sold to third parties. Data is only shared when necessary to provide our services (e.g. to repair service providers for booked repairs) or when we are legally required to do so.

section5.title: 5. Data Security
section5.body: We implement technical and organisational measures to protect your data from unauthorised access, loss or misuse. Passwords are stored exclusively in hashed form.

section6.title: 6. Cookies
section6.body: Our platform uses functional cookies for session management and authentication. Analytical cookies are only set with your consent.

section7.title: 7. Your Rights
section7.downloadText: Registered users can download a complete copy of their data at any time under
section7.contactText: For all other concerns, please contact:

section8.title: 8. Retention Period
section8.body: We store your personal data only for as long as necessary for the stated purposes or as required by legal retention periods. Account and profile data will be removed after account deletion, unless legal obligations (e.g. tax retention of invoices) prevent this.

section9.title: 9. Legal Basis
section9.body: Processing is based on the Swiss Federal Act on Data Protection (FADP) and, where applicable, on Art. 6(1) GDPR (contract performance, legitimate interests, consent).

section10.title: 10. Applicable Law

meta.description: Learn about our exclusive REVAMPED certification – guaranteeing sustainability, quality and performance in every computer we build.

hero.title: The REVAMPED Certification
hero.subtitle: Every computer we build receives our exclusive REVAMPED label – a guarantee of sustainability, quality and performance that sets a new industry standard.

heroSection.title: Real REVAMPED Computers in Action
heroSection.body: Sustainable technology that people love to use. Our REVAMPED certification is not just a promise – it's a visible commitment to quality and environmental responsibility.

whatIs.title: What "REVAMPED" Means
whatIs.body: Our certification is not just a label – it's a comprehensive standard ensuring every computer meets our strict criteria for sustainability, quality and performance.

sustainability.title: Sustainability First
sustainability.body: We prioritize used and refurbished components wherever possible, drastically reducing environmental impact while maintaining peak performance. We follow circular economy principles in every build.

quality.title: Quality Guaranteed
quality.body: Every component is thoroughly tested, cleaned and verified to meet our strict performance and reliability standards. Quality is never compromised for sustainability.

ai.title: AI-Optimized
ai.body: Our AI ensures perfect component compatibility and optimal performance tuning for your specific use case. Every build is intelligently designed for your needs.

certificate.title: Certificate of Authenticity
certificate.subtitle: Every REVAMPED computer comes with a detailed certificate providing full transparency about your sustainable build.
certificate.check2: CO₂ emissions saved vs. new build
certificate.certBody: Your certificate includes both a physical sticker and a digital QR code for instant verification and detailed component information.

stickers.title: The REVAMPED Label Collection
stickers.subtitle: Each design represents our commitment to sustainable computing and quality assurance.

sticker1.title: Original Design
sticker1.body: Clean, professional certification mark emphasizing our laptop-focused sustainability approach

sticker2.title: Eco Variant
sticker2.body: Extended design emphasizing our environmental focus and circular economy principles

sticker3.title: Digital Certificate
sticker3.body: QR-code linked verification system with blockchain-backed authenticity records

cta.title: Join the REVAMPED Revolution
cta.body: When you choose a REVAMPED computer, you're not just getting a great machine – you're making a statement about the future of technology. Sustainable, powerful and built with purpose.

hero.title: Become a member

whatYouGet.title: Voting rights on association decisions
whatYouGet.description: You are automatically invited to votes and help decide the direction of the association.

whatYouGet.title: Official membership list
whatYouGet.description: You are legally part of the association's membership under Swiss association law (ZGB Art. 60 ff).

whatYouGet.title: Financial support
whatYouGet.description: Your annual fee helps us finance the association sustainably — independent of external funders.

items.question: What's the difference between a user account and membership?
items.answer: You need a user account for marketplace, IT help and workshops — it's free. Association membership gives you voting rights and officially makes you part of the membership. Most users will never become members — that's completely fine.

items.question: What do I actually get as a member?
items.answer: Voting rights on association decisions and a place on the official membership list. We don't promise exclusive benefits — our services are for everyone. You become a member because you want to support the association.

items.question: How does joining work?
items.answer: Fill out the form, transfer the annual fee — done. You're a member immediately. No waiting, no approval needed.

items.question: How do I pay the annual fee?
items.answer: After joining, you'll see the bank details directly on the confirmation page. Bank transfer or TWINT.

items.question: Can I leave at any time?
items.answer: Yes. An informal email to the board is sufficient. Already paid annual fees are not refunded.
